description: Phase 1 Foundation + Expression AST - Core types, encoding/decoding, expression AST, CRUD operations, AWS adapter, benchmarks, and README for a generics-first DynamoDB library for Go

userStories.title: Core types and Backend interface
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need the foundational types (DB, Table, Backend interface, AttributeValue, KeyValue, and library-owned request/response structs) so that all subsequent operations have a stable base to build on.
acceptanceCriteria: DB struct exists with a New(backend Backend, opts ...Option) *DB constructor
Table struct exists with db.Table(name string, opts ...TableOption) *Table method
Backend interface defined with all 10 methods: GetItem, PutItem, DeleteItem, UpdateItem, Query, Scan, BatchGetItem, BatchWriteItem, TransactGetItems, TransactWriteItems
All request/response types are library-owned (e.g. GetItemRequest, GetItemResponse) with no AWS SDK types in the interface
AttributeValue type defined as a library-owned sum type (not types.AttributeValue from AWS SDK) supporting S, N, B, BOOL, NULL, L, M, SS, NS, BS
ConsumedCapacity struct defined
KeyValue type defined (opaque type that resolves to map[string]AttributeValue internally)
Key(pairs ...any) KeyValue helper function implemented; accepts 2 args (hash only) or 4 args (hash + range)
Key() panics on odd argument count, non-string attribute names, or unsupported value types (programmer error, same convention as regexp.MustCompile)
Unit tests for Key() construction with string, number, and binary key values, and panic on invalid input
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Struct tag parser
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need a struct tag parser that reads the full dynamo tag syntax so that encoding/decoding and future phases (GSI metadata, key schema) all use a single cached parse result.
acceptanceCriteria: Parses dynamo struct tag (not dynago)
Recognises all tag options: field name, hash, range, gsi:<name>, omitempty, set, unixtime, - (skip)
Stores parsed result in a fieldOptions struct containing all metadata (including GSI/key info that Phase 1 won't act on)
typeCodec struct caches parsed field metadata per struct type via sync.Map
Untagged exported fields default to using the field name as the attribute name
Unexported fields are always skipped
- tag skips the field
Tag parsing is tested for all recognised options including combinations (e.g. dynamo:"GSI1PK,gsi:GSI1,hash")
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Encoding pipeline (Marshal)
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want to marshal Go structs to map[string]AttributeValue so that I can write items to DynamoDB without manual attribute construction.
acceptanceCriteria: Marshal(v any) (map[string]AttributeValue, error) function implemented
Supports string, int/int64/float64 (as DynamoDB N), bool, []byte (as B), time.Time (as N unixtime when tagged, ISO 8601 string otherwise)
Supports slices (as L), maps (as M), nested structs (as M)
Supports []string / []int / []float64 / [][]byte as sets (SS/NS/BS) when tagged with set
Supports pointer fields (nil pointer omitted)
omitempty skips zero-value fields (empty string, 0, false, nil pointer, empty slice/map)
Custom marshaler interface: types implementing MarshalDynamo() (AttributeValue, error) are used
Uses cached typeCodec from US-002 with no re-parsing on repeated calls
Unit tests for every supported type and tag option
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Decoding pipeline (Unmarshal)
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want to unmarshal map[string]AttributeValue into typed Go structs so that I get compile-time safe results from DynamoDB reads.
acceptanceCriteria: Unmarshal(item map[string]AttributeValue, out any) error function implemented
Supports all types from US-003 in reverse direction
time.Time decoded from unixtime (N) when tagged, ISO 8601 (S) otherwise
Sets (SS/NS/BS) decoded into slices when field is tagged with set
Pointer fields: missing attribute sets pointer to nil (does not allocate)
Extra attributes in the map that don't match struct fields are silently ignored
Custom unmarshaler interface: types implementing UnmarshalDynamo(AttributeValue) error are used
Round-trip test: Marshal then Unmarshal produces identical struct for all supported types
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expression AST node types
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need the expression AST node type definitions so that the builder, translator, and evaluator all share the same representation.
acceptanceCriteria: internal/expr/ast.go defines Node interface and node types: CompareNode, LogicalNode, FuncNode, PathNode, ValueNode, UpdateNode, ProjectionNode
CompareOp enum: EQ, NE, LT, LE, GT, GE
LogicalOp enum: AND, OR, NOT
FuncNode supports function names: attribute_exists, attribute_not_exists, begins_with, contains, size
PathNode supports nested paths (e.g. ["Address", "City"])
UpdateNode supports actions: SET, ADD, REMOVE, DELETE
internal/reserved.go contains DynamoDB reserved words list
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Expression AST builder (placeholder parser)
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need builder functions that parse placeholder syntax like 'Active = ? AND Age > ?' into AST nodes so that expressions are constructed from user-friendly strings.
acceptanceCriteria: Builder functions parse ? placeholder syntax: Filter("Active = ? AND Age > ?", true, 21) produces the correct AST
Builder handles #name reserved-word attribute name placeholders
Builder handles function call syntax: begins_with(SK, ?), contains(Tags, ?), attribute_exists(Email)
Builder handles nested path syntax: Address.City = ?
Builder handles compound expressions with AND, OR, NOT and correct precedence
Builder produces UpdateNode from Set(attr, val), Add(attr, val), Remove(attr), Delete(attr, val) calls
Unit tests for AST construction from placeholder expressions including edge cases (nested paths, multiple conditions, function calls)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expression AST DynamoDB string translator
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need to translate the expression AST into DynamoDB expression strings and attribute name/value maps so that the AWS adapter can send expressions to real DynamoDB.
acceptanceCriteria: expr.ToDynamo(node Node) returns (string, map[string]string, map[string]AttributeValue) translating AST to expression string + ExpressionAttributeNames + ExpressionAttributeValues
Reserved-word attributes are automatically aliased in ExpressionAttributeNames (e.g. #Status for Status)
Value placeholders are replaced with :v0, :v1, etc. in ExpressionAttributeValues
Handles compound expressions (AND/OR/NOT) with correct parenthesisation
Handles function calls (begins_with(#path, :v0))
Handles update expressions (SET, ADD, REMOVE, DELETE actions)
Unit tests: each node type translates correctly; round-trip from builder string to AST to DynamoDB string preserves semantics
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expression AST condition/filter evaluator
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need an AST evaluator for condition and filter expressions so that the in-memory backend (Phase 3) can evaluate them directly without parsing DynamoDB expression strings.
acceptanceCriteria: expr.Eval(node Node, item map[string]AttributeValue) (bool, error) evaluates condition/filter expressions
Comparison operators work for strings (lexicographic), numbers (numeric), and binary (byte comparison)
Logical operators (AND, OR, NOT) short-circuit correctly
attribute_exists returns true if path exists (including nested paths), false otherwise
attribute_not_exists is the inverse of attribute_exists
begins_with(path, prefix) works on string values
contains(path, value) works on strings (substring) and lists (element membership)
size(path) returns the size of strings (length), lists (length), maps (key count), binary (byte length)
Nested path traversal: Address.City navigates into map attributes
Unit tests for every operator, function, and type combination
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Expression AST update evaluator
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need an AST evaluator for update expressions so that the in-memory backend (Phase 3) can apply SET, ADD, REMOVE, and DELETE operations directly on items.
acceptanceCriteria: expr.EvalUpdate(node Node, item map[string]AttributeValue) (map[string]AttributeValue, error) applies update expressions to an item
SET action sets an attribute to a value, including nested paths
ADD action adds a number to a numeric attribute, or adds elements to a set
REMOVE action removes an attribute from the item
DELETE action removes elements from a set attribute
Multiple update actions can be applied in a single EvalUpdate call
Returns error for type mismatches (e.g. ADD on a string attribute)
Unit tests for each action type including edge cases (nested paths, sets, missing attributes)
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Error types and sentinel values
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want structured error types so that I can programmatically handle DynamoDB-specific error conditions without string matching.
acceptanceCriteria: errors.go defines sentinel errors: ErrNotFound, ErrConditionFailed, ErrValidation, ErrTransactionCancelled
IsNotFound(err) bool returns true when a Get/Query returns no item
IsCondCheckFailed(err) bool returns true for condition expression failures
IsValidation(err) bool returns true for client-side validation errors (missing key, bad type)
IsTxCancelled(err) bool returns true for transaction cancellation errors
All error types implement error and support errors.Is / errors.As unwrapping
Unit tests for error wrapping, Is, and As behaviour
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Table.Put and Table.Delete methods
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want to put and delete items via methods on *Table so that the type is inferred from the argument and I get editor autocompletion via table.
acceptanceCriteria: table.Put(ctx, item, opts ...PutOption) error marshals the item and calls Backend.PutItem
table.Delete(ctx, key KeyValue, opts ...DeleteOption) error calls Backend.DeleteItem
PutOption supports: IfNotExists(attr), Condition(expr, vals...) which builds AST and translates for AWS
DeleteOption supports: Condition(expr, vals...)
Condition expressions are built via the AST builder and translated via ToDynamo
Put returns ErrConditionFailed when condition fails
Unit tests using a stub backend that captures the request and validates encoding, key construction, and expression translation
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Get[T] free function
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want a generic Get[T] function so that I get a typed result without casting or out-parameters.
acceptanceCriteria: func Get[T any](ctx context.Context, t *Table, key KeyValue, opts ...GetOption) (T, error) implemented
Calls Backend.GetItem, unmarshals response into T
Returns ErrNotFound when item does not exist (response has nil/empty Item) — does not return zero-value T with nil error
GetOption supports: ConsistentRead(), Project(attrs ...string) which builds projection AST
Projection expressions are translated to DynamoDB format for the request
Unit tests with stub backend for success, not-found, and projection cases
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: KeyCondition and Query[T] free function
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want a generic Query[T] with KeyCondition as a required parameter so that the compiler enforces that I always provide a partition key.
acceptanceCriteria: KeyCondition struct with Partition(attr, val) constructor
Sort key modifiers on KeyCondition: SortEquals, SortBeginsWith, SortBetween, SortGreaterThan, SortLessThan, SortGreaterOrEqual, SortLessOrEqual
func Query[T any](ctx context.Context, t *Table, key KeyCondition, opts ...QueryOption) ([]T, error) implemented
QueryOption supports: Filter(expr, vals...), Limit(n), ScanForward(bool), Index(name), Project(attrs...), ConsistentRead()
Key condition is translated to DynamoDB KeyConditionExpression via AST
Filter is translated to FilterExpression via AST
Results are unmarshalled into []T
Pagination: when DynamoDB returns LastEvaluatedKey, the function automatically fetches subsequent pages until all results are collected (up to Limit if set)
Unit tests with stub backend covering: partition-only query, partition+sort, filters, limit, index, pagination across multiple pages
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Scan[T] free function
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want a generic Scan[T] function so that I can scan a table with optional filters and get typed results.
acceptanceCriteria: func Scan[T any](ctx context.Context, t *Table, opts ...ScanOption) ([]T, error) implemented
ScanOption supports: Filter(expr, vals...), Limit(n), Index(name), Project(attrs...), ConsistentRead()
Automatic pagination (same as Query)
Results are unmarshalled into []T
Unit tests with stub backend covering: full scan, filtered scan, limit, pagination
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Table.Update method and UpdateReturning[T] free function
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want table.Update() for fire-and-forget updates (returns only error) and dynago.UpdateReturning[T]() when I need the updated item back as a typed result, eliminating the zero-value trap.
acceptanceCriteria: table.Update(ctx, key KeyValue, opts ...UpdateOption) error is a method on *Table, no type parameter needed, returns only error
func UpdateReturning[T any](ctx context.Context, t *Table, key KeyValue, opts ...UpdateOption) (T, error) is a free function returning typed item
UpdateReturning[T] requires ReturnNew() or ReturnOld() option; returns an error if neither is provided
UpdateOption supports: Set(attr, val), Add(attr, val), Remove(attr), Delete(attr, val) for set removal
UpdateOption supports: IfCondition(expr, vals...) for conditional updates
UpdateOption supports: ReturnNew(), ReturnOld() to control which item state is returned
Update expressions are built via the AST and translated to DynamoDB format
Both functions return ErrConditionFailed when condition fails
Unit tests with stub backend covering: table.Update with SET/ADD/REMOVE/DELETE, UpdateReturning[T] with ReturnNew/ReturnOld, conditional update, missing return option error
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Typed iterators with pagination
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I want typed iterators for Query and Scan so that I can process large result sets without loading everything into memory, using idiomatic Go 1.23 range over func.
acceptanceCriteria: func QueryIter[T any](ctx context.Context, t *Table, key KeyCondition, opts ...QueryOption) iter.Seq2[T, error] returns a Go 1.23+ iterator
func ScanIter[T any](ctx context.Context, t *Table, opts ...ScanOption) iter.Seq2[T, error] returns a Go 1.23+ iterator
Iterators automatically paginate: when a page is exhausted, the next page is fetched using LastEvaluatedKey
Error convention: on a failing iteration, the iterator yields (zeroT, err) as the final element and stops — no separate Err() method
Iteration stops when: all pages are consumed, an error occurs, or the caller breaks out of the range loop
Breaking out of the range loop does not leak goroutines or leave pending requests
Unit tests with stub backend: iterate over multi-page results, break mid-page, error mid-iteration, verify error is yielded as final element
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: AWS adapter — AttributeValue translation
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need translation functions between library-owned AttributeValue and AWS SDK AttributeValue types so that the AWS backend can convert data at the boundary.
acceptanceCriteria: toAWSAttributeValue and fromAWSAttributeValue handle all variants: S, N, B, BOOL, NULL, L, M, SS, NS, BS
toAWSKey and toAWSItem translate map[string]AttributeValue to AWS SDK equivalents
fromAWSItem translates AWS SDK item back to library-owned map[string]AttributeValue
go.mod includes AWS SDK v2 dependencies (github.com/aws/aws-sdk-go-v2/service/dynamodb)
Unit tests for attribute value translation (both directions) covering all DynamoDB types
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: AWS adapter — CRUD backend methods
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I need the AWS adapter to implement GetItem, PutItem, DeleteItem, and UpdateItem so that basic CRUD operations work against real DynamoDB.
acceptanceCriteria: AWSBackend struct with NewAWSBackend(client *dynamodb.Client) *AWSBackend constructor
GetItem method translates GetItemRequest to AWS SDK GetItemInput, calls client, translates response back
PutItem method translates PutItemRequest including condition expressions wired from AST via ToDynamo
DeleteItem method translates DeleteItemRequest including condition expressions
UpdateItem method translates UpdateItemRequest including update expressions and condition expressions
AWS SDK errors are wrapped into library error types: ConditionalCheckFailedException to ErrConditionFailed, ResourceNotFoundException to appropriate error
Unit tests verifying request translation and error wrapping
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: AWS adapter — Query, Scan, Batch, and Transaction methods
userStories.description: As a developer using DynaGo, I need the AWS adapter to implement Query, Scan, BatchGetItem, BatchWriteItem, TransactGetItems, and TransactWriteItems so that the Backend interface is fully satisfied.
acceptanceCriteria: Query method translates QueryRequest including KeyConditionExpression, FilterExpression, ProjectionExpression, and pagination (ExclusiveStartKey / LastEvaluatedKey)
Scan method translates ScanRequest including FilterExpression, ProjectionExpression, and pagination
BatchGetItem method translates BatchGetItemRequest (thin pass-through for now)
BatchWriteItem method translates BatchWriteItemRequest (thin pass-through for now)
TransactGetItems method translates TransactGetItemsRequest (thin pass-through for now)
TransactWriteItems method translates TransactWriteItemsRequest (thin pass-through for now)
AWSBackend fully satisfies the Backend interface — all 10 methods implemented
Unit tests verifying query/scan request translation with expressions and pagination fields
Typecheck passes
Tests pass

userStories.title: Tier 1 benchmarks — encoding, decoding, and cache
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need encoding and decoding benchmarks so that I have performance baselines for the codec from day one.
acceptanceCriteria: BenchmarkEncode_Flat: flat struct with 5 common fields
BenchmarkEncode_Nested: struct with nested structs, slices, maps
BenchmarkEncode_Large: struct with 30+ fields
BenchmarkEncode_WithSets: struct with string sets, number sets
BenchmarkDecode_Flat, BenchmarkDecode_Nested, BenchmarkDecode_Large: reverse of encode benchmarks
BenchmarkEncode_ColdCache: new type each iteration to measure first-parse cost
BenchmarkEncode_WarmCache: same type to measure cached codec performance
All benchmarks use b.ReportAllocs()
All benchmarks pass with go test -bench=. -benchmem ./...
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Tier 1 benchmarks — expression, round-trip, and key construction
userStories.description: As a library developer, I need expression, round-trip, and key construction benchmarks to establish performance baselines for non-codec hot paths.
acceptanceCriteria: BenchmarkExprBuild_SimpleFilter: Filter("Active = ? AND Age > ?", true, 21)
BenchmarkExprBuild_ComplexCondition: 5-6 conditions ANDed with nested paths
BenchmarkExprTranslate_ToDynamo: pre-built AST to DynamoDB expression string
BenchmarkGetRoundTrip: full Get cycle with noop backend returning pre-built response
BenchmarkPutRoundTrip: full Put cycle with noop backend
BenchmarkQueryRoundTrip: full Query cycle with noop backend returning 100-item response
BenchmarkKeyConstruction: Key("PK", UserPK("123"), "SK", OrderSK("2024-01-15", "abc"))
All benchmarks use b.ReportAllocs()
All benchmarks pass with go test -bench=. -benchmem ./...
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Minimal README
userStories.description: As a potential user visiting the repository, I want a README that explains what DynaGo is and shows a basic usage example so that I can quickly understand the project's purpose.
acceptanceCriteria: Project title and one-paragraph description (generics-first, single-table-native, in-memory testing)
Install instructions (go get)
One code example showing Put and Get[T] with a simple struct using dynamo struct tags
Go version requirement noted (1.23+)
Status: Work in Progress note
License mention
File saved as README.md in project root
